items.title: Marilyn's world 'fell apart' with one post. What happened next helped her heal

children.content: Marilyn was just five when she first encountered grief. Now 18, the teenager says she's been to more funerals than birthday parties.

gallery.altText: A young woman with a flower tucked behind her ear smiles as she holds onto her necklace.

thumbnail.altText: A young woman with a flower tucked behind her ear smiles as she holds onto her necklace.

items.title: 'Girl who screams most nights': NT hearings address child protection system

children.content: Some foster carers have voiced support for the NT's proposed changes to child protection laws, but others say the current legislation already outlines individual safety as the paramount consideration.

image.altText: Nine people sitting along a long table, each with microphones before them, facing another table where politicians are sitting.

items.title: Remote community love story told through first-time director's rom-com

children.content: In a First Nations romantic comedy debuting in Sydney this week, a new writer-director has used her lived experience to tell the story of young love in a remote Northern Territory community.

image.altText: A young Indigenous woman looks over her left shoulder towards a shirtless man standing behind her.

items.title: Couple back at Finke Desert Race after finding love via social post

children.content: When motorbike enthusiast Ben Rawcliffe posted on a Finke Desert Race Facebook group looking for his future wife, his post garnered hundreds of comments. Now, he's back in the red centre, supporting his new partner as she takes to the tough off-road track.

articles.title: Finke Desert Race legends reflect on event ahead of 50th anniversary

children.content: The 460-kilometre test of skill and endurance through the central Australian outback has become one of the most famous off-road races in the world.

articles.title: Hollywood legend set to bring Finke Desert Race to the big screen

children.content: The gruelling outback Finke Desert Race is set for Hollywood treatment after Marvel's Chris Hemsworth backs a feature-length movie.

image.altText: Chris Hemsworth stares into the camera.

gallery.altText: A man and a woman in motorbike fear in front of a range with the sun behind.

thumbnail.altText: A man and a woman in motorbike fear in front of a range with the sun behind.

items.title: Country's biggest barra fishing comp under way, with some reporting more crocs

children.content: The country's biggest annual barramundi fishing competition is under way on the Northern Territory's remote Daly River, after the tournament was delayed by six weeks due to extensive flooding.

image.altText: White man standing on boat, wearing long sleeve purple / navy hoodie, holding barramundi fish from belly and mouth, posing

items.title: Kumanjayi Little Baby's family were forced to leave home community, MP says

children.content: Member for Lingiari Marion Scrymgour says the mother of Kumanjayi Little Baby had been living in insecure housing in Alice Springs so she could access specialist support for her daughter, before the five-year-old's tragic death in April.

image.altText: Two women sit opposite each other.

items.title: 'Like opening an oven': NT constable praised for 'heroic' rescue attempt

children.content: A "heroic" Alice Springs police officer who risked his life repeatedly entering a burning building in trying to rescue a three-year-old boy will be awarded a medal of valour.

image.altText: A shirt memorialising Mitchell Thomas that reads In loving memory of Mitchell

items.title: Daly River service providers facing massive clean-up bill after record flood

children.content: Residents of the Northern Territory town of Nauiyu / Daly River have started returning home after spending three months in evacuation centres following extensive flooding.

image.altText: A man in a bright orange shirt sprays the muddy blue wall of a house

items.title: Video shows man escaping court custody in Darwin, triggering police manhunt

children.content: A manhunt is under way in Darwin as police search for Brandt Graham, a 44-year-old who allegedly scaled an enclosed courthouse dock and escaped from custody today.

image.altText: The outside of the ABC Darwin building, looking out towards the Darwin Local Court.

gallery.altText: A man in a green top running across a road, away from a court building, in a CBD.

thumbnail.altText: A man in a green top running across a road, away from a court building, in a CBD.

items.title: 'Cybertongue' could prevent 70 million tonnes of milk waste every year

children.content: About a sixth of the world's milk is wasted, over 150 tonnes every year, but a Canberra company has come up with new technology that could drastically reduce that.

gallery.altText: A line of two-litre milk bottles in a processing facility.

thumbnail.altText: A line of two-litre milk bottles in a processing facility.

items.title: Missing Melbourne teacher allegedly murdered by brother in India

children.content: Indian authorities allege Melbourne maths teacher Sunil Sharma was murdered by his brother and dumped in a canal. He had been in India to prepare a property for sale.

image.altText: Relatives of missing Melbourne man Sunil Sharma who are accused in relation to his disappearance and death

items.title: Multiple people shot near Ohio festival, US authorities say

children.content: Multiple people have been shot near a festival in Ohio, authorities say.

image.altText: Ohio police cordon off the road.

items.title: Ukraine renews attacks on St Petersburg after Putin rejects Zelenskyy  meeting

children.content: A large-scale Ukrainian drone attack targets St Petersburg, with Russia intercepting 376 drones.

image.altText: A plume of black smoke is seen over the port of St. Petersburg, Russia,

items.title: Will Australia be taken for a ride in the AI and data centre boom?

children.content: More than $150 billion is lined up for data centre development and investment in Australia, but there are questions as to whether the boom will provide benefits beyond a short-term construction boost.

image.altText: A data centre

items.title: Struggling and desperate single mum saved by an 'employment mentor'

children.content: Personalised support helped domestic violence survivor and single mother Harsimran get a job, despite significant barriers. It's an approach that could benefit more jobseekers following the federal government's revamp of Workforce Australia.

gallery.altText: Harsimran leaning on a wooden fence on top of a hill with a green landscape behind her.

thumbnail.altText: Harsimran leaning on a wooden fence on top of a hill with a green landscape behind her.

items.title: Boy on bail charged over stabbing of homeless man in Bendigo

children.content: Police have charged a 16-year-old boy who was on bail over the stabbing of a homeless man in Bendigo on Thursday night.

image.altText: CCTV of a street at night time, group of four walking down the road.

items.title: India's 'cockroach' political movement spills onto Delhi's streets

children.content: India's largest online youth movement has taken to the streets for the first time, calling for the resignation of the education minister.

image.altText: Abhijeet Dipke, head of the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP), speaks to supporters of the CJP

items.title: Planning, packing and nerves: Six Aussies kick off Race Around The World

children.content: Six Australian filmmakers are about to embark on the journey of a lifetime on Race Around The World. Here's how they're preparing for the unknown.

image.altText: Six people huddle together and smile

items.title: Get a buzz out of spelling? It's time to show off your skills

children.content: Admit it, you can't get enough of my synonyms and similes. Let's get spelling!

image.altText: Guy Montgomery beams as he stands in front of the Guy Mont set in front of a podium bearing a cup, purse and bucket.

items.title: 30 years of Pokémon: The 'immersive' phenomenon still finding new fans

children.content: Three decades after the franchise that coined the phrase "Gotta catch 'em all!" was first released, parents are sharing their love of the franchise with their children.

items.title: Tasmania's police minister apologises for naming Port Arthur gunman

children.content: Felix Ellis has apologised for comments he made during budget estimates about the 1996 Port Arthur massacre in which he named the gunman, with one survivor labelling his comments "hurtful" and "disrespectful".

image.altText: A bearded man seen in profile.

items.title: Diver dies after being bitten by 4.5m shark off Albany, WA

children.content: A male diver has died after an attack by a suspected 4.5-metre shark near Michaelmas Island, off WA's Albany coast, on Saturday morning.

image.altText: police albany 3

items.title: Shots fired where gangster's funeral was advertised to be held

children.content: The ABC understands the funeral of Sydney gangster Lorenzo Lemalu was originally scheduled to take place at the venue in Punchbowl, but the plans were changed.

image.altText: a window with a hole after it was shot

items.title: Pope competes with Bad Bunny in Spain, urges leaders to reject polarisation

children.content: Pope Leo urges global leaders not to "fan the flames of polarisation" to gain popularity, warning of the artificial environment created by new technologies.

image.altText: Pope Leo stands behind protective glass in the popemobile as it moved through a crowded street escorted by security

items.title: 'We were so close': Laos cave rescue called off as conditions deteriorate

children.content: The search for two men trapped in a flooded cave in Laos has been postponed as rescuers say a "miracle" is needed.

image.altText: Rescuers stand under torchlight near the entrance of a cave in Laos

items.title: 'A very big barrier in life': Learner drivers stuck waiting for help

children.content: A Hobart program helping learner drivers get their provisional licence has a waitlist of more than 100 people.

image.altText: The back of a white Suzuki Swift car has a yellow learner's plate and reads 'Volunteer Driver Mentors Required'.

items.title: Knitters find key to serenity during 'party, party, party' snow season

items.title: Yengi scores on debut as Socceroos draw with Switzerland in WC warm-up

children.content: The Socceroos respond after a poor first half to claim a 1-1 draw against Switzerland in their final match before the FIFA World Cup.

image.altText: Tete Yengi of Australia celebrates with teammates after scoring a goal against Switzerland.

items.title: Mexico score late winner against sloppy Matildas

children.content: The lacklustre Matildas have been beaten by Mexico for the first time in over 20 years, losing 1-0 in Newcastle.

image.altText: Diana Ordonez runs with her arms out

items.title: Fox wins second World Cup gold in two days to continue inspiring comeback

children.content: Three-time Olympic champion Jessica Fox tops the podium again at the Prague leg of the World Cup circuit, only a week after returning to international competition.

image.altText: Jessica Fox celebrates after winning the C1 event at the ICF World Cup event in Prague, Czechia.

items.title: Russian teen Andreeva wins French Open to claim first major title

children.content: Mirra Andreeva defeats surprise finalist Maja Chwalińska 6-3, 6-2 to become the youngest French Open champion in more than three decades.

image.altText: Mirra Andreeva holds the French Open trophy.

items.title: 'It's not normal to be winning the way I'm winning': Johnson excelling in open water

children.content: Moesha Johnson is enjoying an overseas open water swimming adventure that has seen her win gold at every stage. But these are all just stepping stones for the biggest prize of all at the Los Angeles Olympics in 2028.

items.title: Andrew Moloney claims world title two years after quitting in anger

children.content: Andrew Moloney weathered champion Willibaldo Garcia's best over 12 brutal rounds to win a majority decision in their super flyweight world title clash in Japan.

items.title: How the corporate world influenced Diamonds' netball coaching

children.content: The world has never been closer to Australia's Diamonds. Coach Stacey Marinkovich is determined to keep them shining brightest.

image.altText: Stacey Marinkovich gestures while coaching the Diamonds at the 2022 Commonwealth Games.

items.title: Death Cafes help people 'make the most of our finite lives'

children.content: The Death Cafe concept began in Switzerland in the 2000s to help people "make the most of our finite lives". Now, the concept has spread globally.
